Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Starting with the process flow Normally the order would start with RO Then probably check availability CA then reserve items RI then pick PI quality check QC pack PO generate shipping label GSL dispatch D Then maybe invoice IC and record payment RP

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets assume that the standard process is RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In many e commerce scenarios the process is 1 Order received RO 2 Check stock CA 3 Reserve stock RI 4 Pick items PI 5 Pack PO 6 Generate shipping label GSL 7 Dispatch D 8 Invoice IC perhaps after dispatch or before

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Activity Occurrences activ_freq Bounds the number of occurrences for an activity in a case In a typical order each activity happens once So activ_freq maps each activity to 1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Equivalence empty no activities are equivalent Never Together empty no activities cannot co exist

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before If first occurs second must have been executed previously For immediate predecessors CA RO RI CA PI RI QC PI PO QC GSL PO D GSL IC D RP IC Including immediate enforces entire order through chain

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Always After If first occurs second executed in one of the following events Assuming means if first occurs second must occur after Assuming equivalent to reverse of Always Before

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Hmm this is a bit ambiguous Wait maybe translation issue Wait perhaps Always After means if first occurs then second must occur after not necessarily immediately

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Verify with example trace Valid RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP all constraints satisfied Invalid RO RI violates RI CA since CA not occurred before RI
